015 */
016package org.consensusj.jsonrpc;
017
018import org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable;
019
020/**
021 * JSON-RPC returned HTTP status other than 200 (and unfortunately also sometimes when their is response.error)
022 * Additional information is usually in JSON-RPC response
023 * TODO: See TODO in parent class {@link JsonRpcException}
024 * @see org.consensusj.jsonrpc.JsonRpcException
025 */
026public class JsonRpcStatusException extends JsonRpcException {
027    public final String httpMessage;
028    public final int httpCode;
029    public final int jsonRpcCode;
030
031    /**
032     * Http response body as a string. Null if not-available (check deserialized JSON in this case)
033     */
034    @Nullable
035    public final String response;
036
037    /**
038     * Deserialized response message, if available. Null if not-available.
039     * Result is usually null when an error occurs, but the type of the result is
040     * unspecified and could be either {@link java.util.Map}, {@link com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode},
041     * or the result type of the failed request depending upon where and how the exception was
042     * created.
043     */
044    @Nullable
045    public final JsonRpcResponse<?> responseJson;
046
047    /**
048     * Canonical Constructor
049     *
050     * @param message Error message from Json if available, else http status message
051     * @param httpCode HTTP status code, e.g. 404
052     * @param httpMessage HTTP status message, e.g. "Not found" (removed from HTTP/2 and HTTP/3)
053     * @param jsonRPCCode Integer error code in JSON response, if any
054     * @param responseBody responseBody body as string (null if JSON available)
055     * @param responseBodyJson responseBody body as Json Map (null if JSON not-available)
056     */
057    public JsonRpcStatusException(String message, int httpCode, String httpMessage, int jsonRPCCode, @Nullable String responseBody, @Nullable JsonRpcResponse<?> responseBodyJson ) {
058        super(message);
059        this.httpCode = httpCode;
060        this.httpMessage = httpMessage;
061        this.jsonRpcCode = jsonRPCCode;
062        this.response = responseBody;
063        this.responseJson = responseBodyJson;
064    }
065
066    /**
067     * Same as canonical, but without the {@code httpCode} parameter. (which is not present in java.net.http, HTTP/2, etc.)
068     */
069    public JsonRpcStatusException(String message, int httpCode, int jsonRPCCode, @Nullable String responseBody, @Nullable JsonRpcResponse<?> responseBodyJson ) {
070        this(message, httpCode, "", jsonRPCCode, responseBody, responseBodyJson);
071    }
072
073    /**
074     * Constructor for when we were able to deserialize a JSON response
075     * @param httpCode http status code
076     * @param responseBodyJson deserialized JSON
077     */
078    public JsonRpcStatusException(int httpCode, JsonRpcResponse<?> responseBodyJson) {
079        this(responseBodyJson.getError() != null ? responseBodyJson.getError().getMessage() : "",
080                httpCode,
081                responseBodyJson.getError() != null ? responseBodyJson.getError().getCode() : 0,
082                null,
083                responseBodyJson);
084    }
085
086    /**
087     * Constructor for when we were unable to deserialize a JSON response
088     * @param httpCode http status code
089     * @param responseBody response body as a string
090     */
091    public JsonRpcStatusException(int httpCode, String responseBody) {
092        this(responseBody, httpCode, 0, responseBody, null);
093    }
094}
